title: Light Herbal Jamaican Tofu Stir-fry
description: A mild, herb‑infused Jamaican‑style tofu stir‑fry with creamy Monterey Jack, bright peppers and a splash of coconut milk, perfect for a quick, egg‑free lunch.
instructions: Press tofu for at least 20 minutes to remove excess moisture, then cut into 1" cubes.
Combine tofu cubes with 1 tsp dried thyme, ½ tsp allspice, and a pinch of salt; toss gently and set aside.
Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a wok or large skillet over medium‑high heat; add tofu and stir‑fry until golden on all sides, about 5–6 minutes. Remove tofu and set aside.
Add remaining 1 tbsp oil to the pan; sauté onion and bell pepper until softened, 3–4 minutes.
Stir in garlic and ginger; cook for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Return tofu to the pan, pour in coconut milk, and add lime juice; toss to coat.
Sprinkle grated Monterey Jack over the mixture; cover and let melt for 2 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Mix in sliced scallions and adjust seasoning with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve hot over steamed jasmine rice or quinoa if desired, garnished with extra scallions. Enjoy a light, herby Jamaican stir‑fry that’s egg‑free and full of flavor.
